name: QA 1985
description:
PCP QA Test No. 1985

Exercise a pmfind fix - valgrind-enabled variant.



Copyright (c) 2022 Red Hat.  All Rights Reserved.



**To reproduce this test, please run:**

    build/ci/ci-run.py fedora-rawhide-container reproduce

Exercise a pmfind fix - valgrind-enabled variant.

Copyright (c) 2022 Red Hat. All Rights Reserved.

To reproduce this test, please run:

build/ci/ci-run.py fedora-rawhide-container reproduce
statusMessage:
[failed, exit status 124] - output mismatch (see 1985.out.bad)
2,11c2,14
< QA output created by 1986 --valgrind
< === std out ===
< SOURCE HOSTNAME
< === filtered valgrind report ===
< Memcheck, a memory error detector
< Command: pmfind -S -m probe=127.0.0.1/32
< LEAK SUMMARY:
< definitely lost: 0 bytes in 0 blocks
< indirectly lost: 0 bytes in 0 blocks
< ERROR SUMMARY: 0 errors from 0 contexts ...
---
> Aborted                    (core dumped) if $do_valgrind; then
>     _run_valgrind pmfind -S -m probe=127.0.0.1/32;
> else
>     pmfind -S -m probe=127.0.0.1/32;
> fi | _filter
> Aborted                    (core dumped) ./1986 --valgrind | $PCP_AWK_PROG '
> skip == 1 && $1 == "==="       { skip = 0 }
> /^=== std err ===/             { skip = 1 }
> skip == 0              { print }
> skip == 1              { print >>"'$seq_full'" }'
> !!!
> !!! Killed from check by timeout ...
> !!!
Check local PMCD is still alive ...
